zh.lifeSummary: 當John Wright於1548年5月5日出生在英國艾可凱爾維頓 哈契時，他的父親Middle John Wright26歲，母親Mrs. Middle John Wright26歲。他於1568年10月27日與Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1624年8月19日在英國艾可素斯 維亞爾德享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可素斯 維亞爾德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Wright于1548年5月5日出生在英国艾可凱爾維頓 哈契时，他的父亲Middle John Wright26岁，母亲Mrs. Middle John Wright26岁。他于1568年10月27日与Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1624年8月19日在英国艾可素斯 維亞爾德享年76岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可素斯 維亞爾德。

nameMeanings.name: Wright
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a craftsman or maker of machinery, mostly in wood, of any of a wide range of kinds, from Middle English and Older Scots wriht, wright, wricht, writh, write (Old English wyrhta, wryhta) ‘craftsman’, especially ‘carpenter, joiner’. The term is found in various combinations (for example, Cartwright and Wainwright ), but when used in isolation it often referred to a builder of windmills or watermills. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

variants.name: Wrights
meanings: English: variant of Wright , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Wrightsman
meanings: Probably English (although no longer found in Britain), meaning‘servant of the wright’ (see   Wright  ).

variants.name: Cartwright
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): occupational name from Middle English cart(e) ‘cart’ + wright(e) ‘craftsman’ (Old English cræt or Old Norse kartr + Old English wyrhta), for a cartwright, a maker of carts.
Variant of   Courtright  , a surname of Dutch origin.

variants.name: Wrightson
meanings: English (northern England, especially Durham): patronymic from   Wright  , meaning ‘son of the wright’.

variants.name: McWright
meanings: Scottish or Irish: Gaelic patronymic with the English and Scots occupational term   Wright  , meaning ‘son of the wright’. The name is possibly a part translation of   McIntyre  , which fully translates as ‘son of the wright’.

variants.name: Wrightman
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English wright ‘craftsman’ (see Wright ) + man ‘man’, denoting someone who assisted a craftsman.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When John Wright was born on 5 May 1548, in Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England, his father, Middle John Wright, was 26 and his mother, Mrs. Middle John Wright, was 26. He married Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell on 27 October 1568, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 7 daughters. He died on 19 August 1624, in South Weald, Essex, England, at the age of 76, and was buried in South Weald, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Wright est né le 5 mai 1548, à Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Middle John Wright, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Middle John Wright, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell le 27 octobre 1568, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé le 19 août 1624, à South Weald, Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à South Weald, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: John Wrightが1548年5月5日にイギリスエセックスケルベドン ハトチで生まれた時，父Middle John Wrightは26歳，母Mrs. Middle John Wrightは26歳であった。1568年10月27日にイギリスエセックスでJudith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1624年8月19日にイギリスエセックスソウス ウエアルドで76歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスソウス ウエアルドにて埋葬された。

ko.St Peter's Church, South Weald, Essex, E: 영국 에세스 소우스 위드 스트 피터'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: John Wright(이)가 1548년 5월 5일 영국 에세스 켈브던 해트치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Middle John Wright(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Middle John Wright(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1568년 10월 27일 영국 에세스에서 Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1624년 8월 19일 영국 에세스 소우스 위드에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 소우스 위드에 묻혔다.

pt.5 May 1548–19 Aug 1624, May 30: 5 de maio de 1548–19 de agosto de 1624
pt.Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, England: Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Kelvedon Manor, Essex, England, United K: Kelvedon Manor, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom

pt.South Weald, Essex, England: South Weald, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Wright nasceu em 5 de maio de 1548, em Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Middle John Wright, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Middle John Wright, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell em 27 de outubro de 1568, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de agosto de 1624, em South Weald, Essex, Inglaterra, com 76 anos, e foi sepultado em South Weald, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Peter's Church, South Weald, Essex, E: ст питир'с чурч, соут вилд, эссекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Wright родился 5 мая 1548 г. в местности келведон хач, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Middle John Wright было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Middle John Wright, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell 27 октября 1568 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 7 дочерей. Он умер 19 августа 1624 г. в местности соут вилд, эссекс, Англия в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности соут вилд, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Wright nació el 5 de mayo de 1548, en Kelvedon Hatch, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Middle John Wright, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Mrs. Middle John Wright, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Judith Elizabeth Emfell Lindsell el 27 de octubre de 1568, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 19 de agosto de 1624, en South Weald, Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultado en South Weald, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
